articles.title: How early SunOS did diskless workstations before NFS
articles.summary: SunOS, an influential operating system, introduced diskless workstations in the late 1980s. This innovation allowed users to boot and run applications without a physical hard drive. The key technology behind this was Sun's implementation of the Network File System (NFS), which enabled remote access to files over a network. NFS revolutionized the way developers approached file sharing, making it easier to collaborate on projects.

articles.title: Quadrupling code performance with a "useless" if
articles.summary: Quadrupling code performance with a "useless" if | purplesyringa's blog Quadrupling code performance with a "useless" if July 12, 2026 The algorithm described in this post is used for optimal coding order of characters in a domain-specific compressor. It finds the shortest path on a grid by following references from one cell to the last one and computes the best cell based on those references. However, the core issue lies in the loop that populates `next_j` array, which has a time complexity of O(n^2) due to nested loops. This can be improved by using instruction-level parallelism, where multiple instructions are executed simultaneously, allowing for faster execution times even with cache limitations.
